title: Three-Bar Linkage
goal: Visualise the minimal crank-rocker linkage: a ground pivot, one crank, and a rocker arm converting rotation to oscillation.
advantages: Simplest closed-loop mechanism to explain crank-rocker behaviour
disadvantages: Limited control over end-effector trajectories compared with higher-bar linkages
cautions: Keep crank length under the Grashof condition to maintain full rotation
